title: citrus × nobilis subf. kotokan
intro: Citrus × nobilis subf. kotokan, also known as Yuzu, is a citrus plant that is a hybrid of Citrus junos and Citrus reticulata, primarily cultivated in East Asia for its unique flavor and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its high concentration of limonene, a compound with known anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Consuming Yuzu and its products has been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, Yuzu is often used in teas, infusions, and juices, as well as in skincare products due to its antioxidant and anti-aging properties.
